review_text: Easy access for transportation bus and train, very near to nature, lake and vineyards
Completely renovated. Now it has large touch screens for itinerary details
Stunning little village perched above Lake Geneva with sweeping vineyard views. Called the Balcony of the Lake. UNESCO-listed Lavaux vineyard terraces.
A pretty station full of flowers, well decorated, with a magnificent view of the lake
Recommend taking a walk from Chexbres station to St. Saphorin station!

Ticket machine, snack machines, photo booth, phone booth, mailbox on platform 1. Heated waiting room in station building. Clean modern toilets cost 1 CHF. Ramps or stairs, trash cans, benches on both platforms. Large paid parking lot.
Small train station with regular schedule every hour. Destinations: Lausanne, Payerne, Vevey. Two Selecta stations for snacks. Heated waiting room.
Well organized station with very friendly polite and helpful staff. Amazing pharmacy shop at the station.
Nice cute and quick metro station, trains scheduled every approx 4 mins to the main Lausanne train station
Magnificent place in Lausanne by Ouchy Port. Take the metro station close by to move everywhere in Lausanne
A potentially forgettable stop, benefits from proximity to Ouchy
Google is confusing M2 Jordils Metro with Line 2 Jordils Bus station. Bus stop is 100m further on Avenue d'Ouchy.
Beautiful landscaped esplanade as you exit the metro
This station marks the transition. From the main station the subway continues underground from here to the lakeshore.
The metro and bus stops are well equipped for passengers
Half a tunnel station and half an open-air station. Well-maintained. Usual accessibility.
Inconvenient access
Centrally located and well connected metro station. Ticket machines might be out of service at times — install SBB app better
Disaster: 30 minutes of waiting. Outrageous
If you are going to tour Lausanne old city center get off here. After the cathedral you can slowly go down to the central station.
Very well designed from an architectural point of view! Beautiful but complicated without the elevator.
Take the elevator to the top floor and you will see the Lausanne Cathedral. Cross the bridge and arrive in about 6 minutes.
Decorated with drawings of caricaturist Mix et Remix. Most beautiful metro stop in Lausanne.
Nice metro station with elevator with lots of shops nearby and connection to bus 7 and bus 6. Has departure board.
A very low-lying metro station. Strongly recommend using elevator for anyone with limited mobility — large number of stairs.
Horrible stop. Traffic lights at intersection take 5 minutes to turn green.
Hospital and the people that work there are amazing. Nothing to complain.
Nice spot! Very well located. Migros, Coop, Fleur de Pain bakery, kiosk, and metro all nearby. Benches to sit and wait for the bus.
They finally added lights to the brown block giving it beauty. Decoration is supposed to be linden leaves. Great Migros and Coop, wide selection.
We often set this metro station as meeting point before driving to activities outside Lausanne. Short distance from motorway.
Jordil's little sister. Terrible stop — nothing there unless you live there.
Large parking lot above. Top floor is aquarium hotel and restaurant.
Without Aquatis and Unisanté this stop is miserable!
Clean and well-organized. Near P+R car park, Acquatis, motorway, and Vennes vocational school.
One of the best and most convenient metro stations!
Very useful metro to Lausanne Flon.
There are not enough sitting places available when waiting.
Too dangerous
Forgettable but there are buses so it's ok if you remember to get off
For three days the elevators not working if you call them from the bridge. One has to walk down to first floor and descend three flights of stairs. Zero accessibility.
Elevators always broken
Not a nice metro station in Lausanne. This one is functional.
Very dirty and unfriendly place at night. Always littering and unsafe. Strong smell of pee.
Too expensive to store a bike there. Not how to encourage bike mobility.
Unbelievable! As soon as it rains this station turns into a sieve! Nobody does anything about it.
Often no train driver. Often only 4 carriages not 8. Often trains replaced by buses.
Good train service. Bad attention from tourist service staff — answered in derogatory way about not speaking English.
Nice city centre with a lot of restaurants and shops.
Nice ferry to ride — you can see the lake. It is also free to take it.
Beautiful lake in front of you. Different activities scattered around and many food stops.
Took 3 hour return cruise. Waitress greeted us asking about eating. Shown to 2nd class dining area. Tables set for lunch. Clean view of the lake. Cosy radiator at our table.
Staff refused to speak English, continuing to speak French. Service was nonexistent. While every French-speaking table was served we were completely ignored for 2.5 hours.
Employee on board began shouting in French. When asked about English he screamed louder. Shocking display of hostility.
Convenient and pretty quay to get to Yvoire. We were lucky enough to board this magnificent boat L'Italie.
A very beautiful clean and quiet place. View of Alps mountains whose peaks are partially covered in snow.
Boat tour had 8 stops over three hours including light lunch. Great service and attention to details.
Very friendly service and also very luxurious and clean ship experience. First class is highly recommended!
Fascinating views of the terraces of Lavaux from the lake. Boats are clean and well maintained. Small cafe inside. Gets filled quickly in a few stops.
At the CGN landing stage you can watch wonderful ships arriving and departing during summer months — unforgettable trip on Lake Geneva.
Two landing stages linked by a promenade that is always flowery. Sometimes vegetable and aromatic herb planters to pick in the early morning.
Beautiful amphitheatrically located town above Lake Leman. Due to Jazz Festival temporary buildings right on the promenade.
Very nice and beautiful place. The Xmas market is always very nice with Santa's show and great food and drinks.
A small convenient port close to the train station offering some great destinations.
A classic tourist trap. Tickets were for round trip from Lausanne not from Villeneuve. Workers dropped us off and told us to run to catch the train.
A beautiful starting point to discover the Lavaux a UNESCO World Heritage site!
The little park next door is the best. So nice to just sit on a bench by the lake and watch the water splash around.
A truly wonderful peaceful place with breathtakingly beautiful panorama. Walk along the shores of Lake Geneva far from hustle and bustle of the city.
Arrived at 12:38am on Monday to a ghost station. Toilets were locked as was the waiting area. Don't understand why stations aren't 24/7 in summer.
Beautiful station with many shops and direct connection to most major towns in Switzerland.
Having to pay for the toilet was surprising. Can't believe they charge for something that should be a basic public service.
Big main station. Several shops coffee cafes mini marts. Elevators available. Metro station very close. Ticket machines on several spots.
